_patch_meta.description: VC session vc-134 — one Session B observation raised: registry composition (ANAKAIN + CHALOPH under 'renewal') with only 3 of 7 OWNER terms surviving the VC filter. Inner-being content in the registry rests primarily on the Greek pair plus H2487.

record.flag_label: Registry 134 'renewal' heterogeneous: ANAKAIN+CHALOPH; only G0341, G0342, H2487 survive VC filter
record.description: Registry 134 (renewal) aggregates two distinct root families under the English label 'renewal': the Greek ANAKAIN family (G0341 anakainoō 'to renew', G0342 anakainōsis 'renewal') and the Hebrew CHALOPH family rooted in cha.laph 'to pass / to change' (H2475 destruction, H2487 change, H2500 preposition 'in return for', H4252 knife/censer, H4253 hair-lock). After VC re-evaluation under v3_4, only three terms retain any inner-being classification: G0341 (group 6132-001, anchor 2Cor 4:16), G0342 (group 1097-001, anchor Rom 12:2), and H2487 (group 1098-001, anchor Job 14:14). The remaining four Hebrew terms (H2475, H2500, H4252, H4253) have no active anchor post-VC; H2475 and H4253 dissolved prior active groups as wrong_face revisions. Session B question: does the registry carry coherent inner-being content for the human inner-being programme, or is 'renewal' in this registry primarily served by the Greek ANAKAIN pair, with the Hebrew CHALOPH root family contributing only through H2487 (change/renewal)? Consider whether the registry composition or scope needs re-examination at the programme-orientation level.

operations.description: SB observation: registry 134 composition — only 3 of 7 OWNER terms carry inner-being function post-VC
